---
title: "Home"
toc: false
format: html
---


:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="35%"}

![](files/images/unm_free_personal_headshot.jpg){.profile-photo}

:::

::: {.column width="65%"}

# Ryan T. Dobson

Ph.D. Student, Evolution and Society Area  
Department of Psychology  
University of New Mexico

::: {.profile-links}
[CV](files/Ryan_Dobson_CV_march15_2026.pdf)
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=y7DOvyMAAAAJ&hl=en)
[GitHub](https://github.com/ryandobson)
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ryan-dobson-2022abc)
[Email](mailto:rdobson@unm.edu)
:::

:::

::::

## About

I am a Ph.D. student in the Evolution and Society area of the Department of Psychology at the University of New Mexico. My research focuses on the evolutionary and developmental foundations of human social relationships and sexual behavior. Much of my work examines cross-sex friendships, relationship dynamics, and the developmental pathways that shape variation in sexual behavior.

I am broadly interested in integrating multiple methodological approaches—including genetically informed designs, longitudinal and intensive longitudinal data, and hormonal measures—to better understand how biological and environmental processes jointly influence human social and mating behavior.

## Explore

You can find more information about my work elsewhere on this site:

- [Research](research.qmd) – publications and ongoing projects  
- [Unpublished Analyses](unpublished_analyses.qmd) – exploratory analyses and research notes  
- [Code](code.qmd) – statistical tools and my R package *codechest*
